zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Bellingham於1452年出生在英國英格蘭素可時，她的父親Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster13歲，母親Joan Wiltshire16歲。她於1504年與Sir Ralph Shirley Lord of Wiston在英國英格蘭素可維斯頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和7個女兒的父母。她去世於1510年之後在英國英格蘭素可維斯頓，埋葬在英國素可米爾格洛福。

nameMeanings.name: Bellingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places called Bellingham, in Greater London (formerly in Kent) and Northumberland. The former is named with Old English Beringahām ‘homestead (Old English hām) of the followers of Be(o)ra’, a byname meaning ‘bear’; the latter seems to have been originally named as the ‘homestead of the dwellers at the bell’, from Old English belle used in a transferred sense of a bell-shaped hill.

History: Richard Bellingham (c. 1592–1672) came from Boston, Lincolnshire, England, to Boston, MA, in 1634. He was a controversial political figure in the new colony, an opponent of John Winthrop (see Winthrop ). He was elected governor of MA in 1641 and again in 1654 and 1665–72. — An Irish family of this name trace their descent from William de Bellingham, who was sheriff of Tynedale, Northumbria, in 1279. They were established in Ireland by Robert Bellingham, who settled in County Longford in 1611. They gave their name to Castlebellingham in County Louth, Ireland.

variants.name: Winthrop
meanings: English (Cumberland and Durham): habitational name from any of the places in Lincolnshire and Nottinghamshire called Winthorpe. The former is named with the Old English personal name or byname Wine, meaning ‘friend’, + Old Norse thorp ‘settlement’. The Lincolnshire placename probably derives from the Old English personal name Wine + Old Norse thorp ‘secondary settlement, outlying farmstead’. The Nottinghamshire placename derives from the Old English personal name Wīgmund or the Old Norse personal name Vígmundr + Old Norse thorp.

History: John Winthrop (1588–1649) was the first governor of the Massachusetts Bay Colony. He kept a detailed journal, an invaluable source for historians. He was born into a gentry family in Suffolk, England, whose fortunes were founded by his grandfather Adam Winthrop (died 1562) of Lavenham. In 1544 the latter acquired a 500-acre estate that had been part of the monastery of Bury St. Edmunds. John Winthrop was a Puritan who emigrated from Groton, Suffolk, to Salem, MA, in 1630 because of Charles I's anti-Puritan policies. By the time of his death he had had four wives and 16 children, the most notable of whom was his son John (1606–76), a scientist and governor of CT. His descendants were prominent in politics and science, including John Winthrop (1714–79), an astronomer, and Robert Winthrop (1809–94), a senator and speaker of the US House of Representatives.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Bellingham was born in 1452, in Sussex, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster, was 13 and her mother, Joan Wiltshire, was 16. She married Sir Ralph Shirley Lord of Wiston in 1504, in Wiston, Sussex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 7 daughters. She died after 1510, in Wiston, Sussex, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Michelgrove, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Bellingham est née en 1452, à Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster, avait 13 ans et sa mère, Joan Wiltshire, avait 16 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Ralph Shirley Lord of Wiston en 1504, à Wiston, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 7 filles. Elle est décédée après 1510, à Wiston, Sussex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Michelgrove, Sussex, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1504 in Wiston, Sussex, England, United Kingdom Sir Ralph Shirley Lord of Wiston geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1510 in Wiston, Sussex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Michelgrove, Sussex, England begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Bellingham(이)가 1452년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster(은)는 13세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Wiltshire(은)는 16세였다. 그녀는 1504년 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 와이스턴에서 Sir Ralph Shirley Lord of Wiston(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1510년 이후 영국 잉글랜드 수세스 와이스턴에서 사망했으며 영국 수세스 믹헬그로브에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir Ralph Shirley Lord of Wiston em 1504, em Wiston, Sussex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 7 filhas. ru.West Grinstead, West Sussex, England, Un: вест гринстид, вест суссекс, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Bellingham родилась в 1452 г. в местности суссекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Bellingham of Lyminster было 13 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Wiltshire, 16. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Ralph Shirley Lord of Wiston в 1504 г. в местности вистон, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 7 дочерей. Она умерла после 1510 г. в местности вистон, суссекс, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности мишелгров, суссекс, Англия.
